fields.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE

## TDD Requirement
Task 53a0-e084 creates a RED test checking for 'consumer_completeness' JSON schema key. After this task:
- test_scope_reviewer_has_consumer_completeness_dimension: passes GREEN

## Implementation Steps
1. Edit plugins/dso/skills/shared/docs/reviewers/scope.md:
   a. Add 4th row to ## Your Dimensions table (after 'dependency_aware' row):
      | consumer_completeness | All consumers of artifacts created/modified by this epic (discovered by Part C scan in epic-scrutiny-pipeline) are covered by success criteria; any uncovered consumer is either explicitly descoped or addressed | Part C scan reveals uncovered consumers and no descoping rationale exists; score N/A when Part C scan was skipped (no consumers found outside own directory) |
   b. In the JSON schema block (lines 52-58), make TWO changes:
      - Add trailing comma to dependency_aware line:
        Before: "dependency_aware": "<integer 1-5 | null>"
        After:  "dependency_aware": "<integer 1-5 | null>",
      - Add new key on the next line (2-space indent, no trailing comma):
          "consumer_completeness": "<integer 1-5 | null>"
      Final JSON block:
        "dimensions": {
          "right_sized": "<integer 1-5 | null>",
          "no_overlap": "<integer 1-5 | null>",
          "dependency_aware": "<integer 1-5 | null>",
          "consumer_completeness": "<integer 1-5 | null>"
        }

2. Edit plugins/dso/skills/shared/workflows/epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:
   a. Line 202 Scope row — EXACT before/after:
      Before: | Senior Product Strategist | `plugins/dso/skills/shared/docs/reviewers/scope.md` | `"Scope"` | `right_sized`, `no_overlap`, `dependency_aware` |
      After:  | Senior Product Strategist | `plugins/dso/skills/shared/docs/reviewers/scope.md` | `"Scope"` | `right_sized`, `no_overlap`, `dependency_aware`, `consumer_completeness` |
   b. Line 196 invocation note — EXACT before/after:
      Before: 2. Pass: the epic title, Context section, Success Criteria, Scenario Analysis section (from Step 3, if present), and (for Scope reviewer) titles of other open epics
      After:  2. Pass: the epic title, Context section, Success Criteria, Scenario Analysis section (from Step 3, if present), and (for Scope reviewer) titles of other open epics and Part C covered_by_SC scan output (file_path, matching_line, covered_by_SC tuples)

3. Update .test-index TWO changes:
   a. Remove the RED marker line added by task 53a0-e084:
      Remove: plugins/dso/skills/shared/docs/reviewers/scope.md:tests/scripts/test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh [test_scope_reviewer_has_consumer_completeness_dimension]
   b. Append tests/scripts/test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh to line 316:
      Before: plugins/dso/skills/shared/docs/reviewers/scope.md:tests/scripts/test-sprint-review-scope-check.sh,tests/scripts/test-isolation-rule-no-unscoped-export.sh
      After:  plugins/dso/skills/shared/docs/reviewers/scope.md:tests/scripts/test-sprint-review-scope-check.sh,tests/scripts/test-isolation-rule-no-unscoped-export.sh,tests/scripts/test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh
      Result: .test-index returns to 2 entries for scope.md

4. Verify structural test passes GREEN

## File Impact
- plugins/dso/skills/shared/docs/reviewers/scope.md: modify (4th table row + JSON comma + JSON key)
- plugins/dso/skills/shared/workflows/epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md: modify (lines 202 and 196)
- .test-index: remove RED marker line; append test file to line 316

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: SERIAL_SUITES=1 bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] consumer_completeness JSON schema key exists in scope.md
  Verify: awk '/"dimensions": \{/,/^\}/' plugins/dso/skills/shared/docs/reviewers/scope.md | grep -q '"consumer_completeness":'
- [ ] structural test GREEN
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh
- [ ] .test-index has exactly 2 entries for scope.md
  Verify: grep -c 'scope.md:' .test-index | grep -q '^2$'
